recheck of final-issues-events fail ('no openable chip on workspace-no-pingpong'): SAMPLE issue, not a regression — that reading's filer 6d077b83 (final-issues-events worker) is CLOSED, and per expected an offline filer stays a non-clickable label: real browser shows SPAN.fv-originator.offline, cursor auto, force-click leaves hash unchanged. On a sample the scenario actually asks for (latest reading filed by a LIVE session — the fresh session-activity readings by aaae1a3e): chip renders BUTTON.fv-originator.alive.openable, click routes to #/sessions/aaae1a3e-… (never #/sessions/new) and the console selects that session (its live terminal feed shown). Both halves of expected hold; the event-detail-fixes navigation fix is intact (draft-leak same batch passed). 0 page errors
switching the selected eval clears the rail composer immediately — an anchored/circled-frame draft AND a plain draft both vanish, so the old eval's remark cannot post to the new thread.
sending an `@new` eval remark briefly flashes the returned mention outcome (`@ new→<session>`) as a page notice above the eval list, matching the Issues page.
clicking the live filer/originator chip navigates to `#/sessions/<session-id>` and selects that session's console — never `#/sessions/new`; offline/missing filers stay non-clickable labels.
